## Prompt:
[

![](data:image/png;base64,)Upwork

](https://www.upwork.com/nx/find-work/)

can you identify and make a list of all proper nouns on this page

## Response:
List of proper nouns extracted from the current Upwork “Find Work” page context:

**Platform & Section Names**

*   Upwork
*   Dashboard
*   Find Work
*   Saved Jobs
*   Proposals and Offers
*   Project Dashboard
*   Direct Contracts
*   Financial Overview
*   Work Diary
*   Reports
*   Certificate of Earnings
*   Help Center
*   Support Requests
*   Upwork Updates
*   Release Notes
*   User Agreement
*   Terms of Use
*   Terms of Service

**Companies / Brands / Products**

*   YouTube
*   Five9
*   Vicidial
*   Referso
*   CliqFit
*   WooCommerce
*   Klaviyo
*   Vert Energy Group
*   Supabase
*   FastAPI
*   HubSpot CRM
*   Apple Search Ads
*   TubeBuddy
*   vidIQ
*   Google Analytics

**Job Titles / Roles (treated as proper nouns when capitalized job postings)**

*   YouTube Editor for Self-Development Channel
*   Podcast Outreach & Partnerships Manager
*   Organic Viral Storytelling Specialist
*   Performance Marketing Strategist/Manager
*   Experienced Bookkeeper with QuickBooks Expertise
*   Klaviyo Expert Needed for Premium D2C Food Brand
*   Expert Klaviyo Email Agency
*   Sales Manager Needed for SaaS Company Fighting Climate Change
*   Front End Programmer – Next.js / React / TypeScript
*   YouTube Monetization Expert

**People & Accounts Mentioned**

*   Todd Nelson
*   Ti Solutins (Client)
*   Uma (Chat with Uma virtual assistant)

**Technologies / Frameworks / Platforms**

*   Next.js
*   React
*   TypeScript
*   Tailwind CSS
*   Python
*   FastAPI
*   Supabase
*   LinkedIn
*   TikTok
*   X (formerly Twitter)
*   Reddit
*   Instagram
*   Facebook
*   YouTube Shorts
*   Apple
*   Canva
*   Figma

**Geographic / Regional References**

*   United States
*   Canada
*   Serbia
*   New York
*   Asia
*   Philippines
*   India
*   Pakistan
*   Bangladesh
*   Eastern Europe
*   Ukraine
*   Serbia (again)
*   Georgia
*   South America
*   Brazil
*   Argentina
*   Colombia

**Legal / Compliance References**

*   CA Notice at Collection
*   Privacy Policy
*   Cookie Policy
*   Accessibility

**Miscellaneous Entities**

*   Upwork Foundation
*   Enterprise Solutions
*   Inc. 5000
*   iTunes
*   Google Play

Would you like me to export these grouped entities into a CSV or table format for sorting/filtering?

## Prompt:
[

![](data:image/png;base64,)Upwork

](https://www.upwork.com/nx/find-work/)

what is Vert Energy Group

## Response:
Vert Energy Group
-----------------

![https://climatebaseuserstorage.s3-us-west-1.amazonaws.com/photos/b777c641-a9c0-4ee4-8d53-f25c2c208c84-DWF6SyQh_400x400.webp](https://climatebaseuserstorage.s3-us-west-1.amazonaws.com/photos/b777c641-a9c0-4ee4-8d53-f25c2c208c84-DWF6SyQh_400x400.webp)

![https://vertenergygroup.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/11/dashboardimg.png](https://vertenergygroup.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/11/dashboardimg.png)

![https://b3310637.smushcdn.com/3310637/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/6c2fc4c8-4c13-434a-b695-69e8aab4e1bb.jpeg?lossy=1&strip=1&webp=1](https://b3310637.smushcdn.com/3310637/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/6c2fc4c8-4c13-434a-b695-69e8aab4e1bb.jpeg?lossy=1&strip=1&webp=1)

Here’s a factual summary of Vert Energy Group (VEG):

### Overview

*   Founded in 2009 (originally as “Complete Green Solutions, Inc.”) and re-branded to “Vert Energy Group” around 2013. [Vert Energy Group+1](https://vertenergygroup.com/about-us/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
*   Headquarters: Irvine, California, USA. [Vert Energy Group+1](https://vertenergygroup.com/booking/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
*   Industry: Commercial real estate / building services – specializing in energy compliance, auditing, benchmarking, modernization, and turn-key engineering services. [Vert Energy Group+1](https://vertenergygroup.com/services/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

### Core Products & Services

*   **Benchmarking and compliance**: They assist building owners/managers with compliance under local/state energy disclosure or benchmarking laws. Their SaaS platform VertPro® supports that. [Vert Energy Group+1](https://vertenergygroup.com/about-us/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
*   **Energy audits, retro-commissioning, building modernization**: They deliver full engineering, project design, commissioning, and upgrades. [Vert Energy Group+1](https://vertenergygroup.com/services/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
*   **Turn-key implementation & owner’s technical representation**: They act as the owner’s representative to manage contractors and ensure project quality. [Vert Energy Group+1](https://vertenergygroup.com/services/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

### Key Differentiators & Market Position

*   They claim to manage compliance for “50+ energy laws” nationwide via their VertPro platform. [Vert Energy Group+1](https://vertenergygroup.com/top-5-reasons-to-choose-vert-energy-group-for-your-next-building-upgrade/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
*   Recognized for growth: On the Inc. 5000 list for multiple consecutive years – a signifier of rapid growth in their segment. [Washington CBPS+1](https://washingtoncbps.com/about?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
*   Their mission emphasizes making compliance and building upgrades “easy” through automation, tools, and managed services. [Vert Energy Group](https://vertenergygroup.com/about-us/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

### Why It Matters / Use-Case

If you manage or own commercial real estate (office buildings, multi-family, industrial), especially in jurisdictions with mandatory energy disclosure/benchmarking laws, partnering with Vert Energy Group could:

*   Reduce risk of non-compliance or late-filing penalties
*   Provide actionable insights on inefficiencies via audits and benchmarking
*   Enable you to plan and execute upgrades in a structured way, potentially improving asset value and reducing operating cost

### Considerations / Risks

*   As with any service provider, verify their track record in _your specific jurisdiction_ (because laws differ substantially by city/state).
*   Their solution combines SaaS + managed services: check how much your organization must invest (time, cost) versus simply software.
*   Upgrades and audits often give ROI over years: ensure financial case is clear for your portfolio.
*   If your assets are outside the U.S. (or in rural/low-size markets) their national scale may not always translate to local expertise — confirm local regulatory knowledge.

* * *

If you like, I can pull up **recent case-studies** of Vert Energy Group (say 2023-2025) including client names, measured savings, and market expansion (which would be useful if you’re assessing their suitability for your portfolio). Would that be useful?

## Prompt:
[

![](data:image/png;base64,)Upwork

](https://www.upwork.com/nx/find-work/)

yes

## Response:
Here are some recent case-studies and documented results for Vert Energy Group (VEG), with notes on outcomes, context and caveats. Use them as starting points — you’ll likely need to verify details if you’re considering them for a serious engagement.

* * *

### ✅ Select Case-Studies / Success Stories

1.  **Portfolio-wide compliance for a 600-warehouse property owner**
    *   A large client with “more than 600 warehouse properties across the U.S.” adopted VEG’s platform VertPro® to shift from reactive to proactive compliance (after receiving violation notices under Los Angeles Existing Buildings Energy & Water Efficiency Ordinance). [Energy Tech Review](https://www.energytechreview.com/vert-energy-group?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
    *   Outcome: Enhanced visibility of which buildings fall under which regulation; reduced risk of penalties; easier sourcing of local vendors. (Quantified savings not publicly detailed beyond risk mitigation)
    *   Takeaway: Good for large multi-site operators who struggle with regulatory complexity.
2.  **$100K+ annual savings uncovered via VertPro data**
    *   According to an overview article, one property manager used VertPro to “uncover $100,000+ in potential annual savings across multiple sites” by analyzing compliance data. [Energy Audit and Compliance+1](https://energy-audit-and-compliance.energytechreview.com/vendor/how-vertpro-transforms-compliance-from-chore-to-competitive-edge--cid-1793-mid-149.html?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
    *   Context: The savings were _potential_ (identified opportunities) rather than guaranteed delivered savings; specifics of asset size, context not disclosed.
    *   Takeaway: The platform claims value beyond just compliance — in cost reduction — though you’ll need to ask for concrete delivered metrics.
3.  **Success stories page – “Pacific Prime Properties” & “Broadway Stages”**
    *   On VEG’s website success-story page: clients such as Pacific Prime Properties and Broadway Stages mention using VertPro. [Vert Energy Group](https://vertenergygroup.com/success-story/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
    *   Detail: The page lists the clients but gives minimal quantitative outcome metrics publicly.
    *   Takeaway: Good reference that the company serves recognized firms, but limited public data on the scale of impact.
4.  **Recognition & scale metrics**
    *   According to a partner site: VEG has “5 consecutive years on Inc. 5000” and their platform manages **10,000+ properties nationwide** managing “50+ energy laws”. [Washington CBPS+1](https://washingtoncbps.com/about?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
    *   Takeaway: Demonstrates scale and credibility. Helps if you’re vetting them for large portfolios.

* * *

### ⚠️ Important Caveats / Risk Considerations

*   Many outcomes are described as “potential savings” or represent identified opportunity rather than guaranteed delivered results.
*   Publicly available case-studies lack full transparency on costs, timelines, ROI, asset types, and baseline conditions — you’ll need to request detailed references for assets comparable to yours.
*   Their value proposition is strong for compliance risk mitigation and portfolio logistics; if your need is more for deep retrofit engineering with large CAPEX, you’ll want to verify their track record in that scope.
*   Jurisdictional variation: Since energy benchmarking & audit laws vary significantly by city/state (e.g., New York City Local Law 97 vs California vs Washington), ensure they have strong local regulatory experience for your region.
*   Because you flagged in your instructions: if their service involves proprietary or sensitive data (they are managing your portfolio energy data, compliance filings) ensure your contract covers confidentiality, data ownership, IP, and performance guarantees.

* * *

If you like, I can **pull together a full list of 5-10 detailed case-studies** (with property type, geography, savings delivered, timeline, client name) including those behind paywalls or industry reports — so you can appraise VEG’s fit for your specific portfolio.

## Prompt:
[

![](data:image/png;base64,)Upwork

](https://www.upwork.com/nx/find-work/)

what is their revenue annually

## Response:
I found conflicting and limited data on the annual revenue of Vert Energy Group:

*   One source states “operating with revenues up to **$10 million**”. [LeadIQ](https://leadiq.com/c/vert-energy-group/5a1daa8c23000054009f2e15?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
*   Another lists revenue at **$1.8 million** as of September 2025. [LeadIQ](https://leadiq.com/c/vert-energy-group/5a1daa8c23000054009f2e15?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
*   A third indicates revenue at **$5.6 million** (older data). [datanyze.com+1](https://www.datanyze.com/companies/vert-energy-group/356287794?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
*   The company is private and does **not** publicly disclose audited revenue figures.

If you like, I can check for the most recent financial filing (if any) or compile an estimate based on comparable firms.

## Prompt:
[

![](data:image/png;base64,)Upwork

](https://www.upwork.com/nx/find-work/)

What is their total available market cap based on the properties that would have value in the platform and the areas they're able to service

## Response:
Estimating the total available market (TAM) for Vert Energy Group (VEG) based on its serviceable domain involves several assumptions. Below is a breakdown of the logic, estimates, and caveats.

* * *

### Assumptions & serviceable domain

*   VEG claims to support portfolio-scale clients and their SaaS platform (VertPro®) manages “10,000+ properties nationwide”. [Washington CBPS](https://washingtoncbps.com/about?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
*   They cover “50+ energy laws” across U.S. jurisdictions. [Washington CBPS+1](https://washingtoncbps.com/about?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
*   Their addressable market is commercial/multifamily real estate subject to energy benchmarking, audit and retrofit mandates (e.g., municipal/state disclosure laws).
*   A rough market sizing approach: number of covered buildings × average spend per building for services (benchmarking + audit + upgrade pipeline + SaaS) × frequency of mandate cycles.

* * *

### Estimate of TAM

1.  **Number of buildings**
    *   Suppose the U.S. has ~ 500,000 large commercial/multi-family buildings subject to such laws (based on retrofit/benchmark industry data). (For example, deep retrofit research shows ~500,000 properties benchmarked as of 2016) [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deep_energy_retrofit?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
    *   Let’s assume ~ 300,000 of those are in VEG’s serviceable size/portfolio scope.
2.  **Average spend per building**
    *   Benchmarking + compliance + audit + upgrades might vary widely. Suppose a conservative average spend of **US$10,000 annually** per building (for smaller buildings) and up to US$100,000+ for large portfolios; let’s pick ~US$20,000 as a mid-point.
3.  **TAM calculation**
    $$
     \text{TAM} \approx 300{,}000 \text{ buildings} \times \$20{,}000/\text{building/year} = \$6\,\text{billion per year}
    $$
    *   If we assume a larger average spend (say $50,000) or more buildings, TAM might be $15 billion+.

* * *

### Summary & caveats

*   My best estimate: **~US$5-10 billion annually** in TAM for VEG’s target domain under current U.S. mandate conditions.
*   This is **not** the current revenue of VEG but the size of the addressable market.
*   Major caveats:
    *   The average spend per building is speculative.
    *   Not all buildings will use a single vendor; competition reduces share.
    *   Mandates and upgrade cycles differ by jurisdiction.
    *   International expansion (if applicable) would increase TAM but also add complexity.
    *   VEG’s effective serviceable share of the market may be much smaller than TAM.

* * *

If you like, I can attempt a **bottom-up estimate using real building counts per jurisdiction (e.g., NYC, LA, Washington State)** to refine the TAM for VEG with more granularity.
